market_summary: FOMC day: Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75% with historic 8-4 dissent (last 4-dissent was Oct 1992). Powell announced he will stay on Board of Governors indefinitely. Oil surged 6%+ as Trump announced extended Iran blockade — Brent at $118, WTI at $107. Dow fell 280pts (-0.57%) for 5th straight decline. S&P and Nasdaq flat. Big Tech (GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, META) reporting after bell. Gold at $4,520, silver at $72.81. BTC holding $77.5K, ETH at $2,330. MSTR at $161-172, Strategy now holds 818K BTC.
key_events_tomorrow: Q1 2026 GDP Advance Estimate — GDPNow 1.2% vs market expectation 2.5-3.0%. MAJOR potential shock if negative.
March PCE Price Index (Fed preferred inflation gauge) — last core PCE at 2.7%, above 2% target. Simultaneous with GDP at 8:30 ET.
Big Tech earnings reactions — GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, META all reported after hours tonight. Market moves depend on results.
Pershing Square IPO day 2 — $5B raised, priced at low end. Watch for secondary market reaction.
overnight_risks: Iran blockade escalation — oil at $118 Brent. Any military escalation or failed negotiations could spike to $130+.
GDP shock risk — GDPNow at 1.2% while market expects 2.5-3.0%. If actual comes in below 1%, recession fears spike.
Big Tech earnings miss — any disappointment from GOOGL/MSFT/AMZN/META could trigger tech selloff.
Fed dissent aftermath — 4-way dissent creates policy uncertainty. Markets may reprice rate path.
Powell staying creates ambiguity about Warsh confirmation and Fed leadership transition.
PCE inflation above expectations would cement higher-for-longer rates narrative.

instrument_news.BTC-USD: Holding $77.5K, up from $76.3K open. Strategy (MSTR) bought 3,273 BTC ($255M) this week, now holds 818K BTC (4% circulating supply). Total raised $61.8B at avg $75,537/coin. 9.6% BTC yield YTD. BTC resilient despite oil shock — potential safe haven narrative strengthening.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: At $2,330, holding above $2,300 support. Values falling earlier this week ahead of Fed. No catalyst for breakout yet — ETH/BTC ratio continues structural decline. Pectra upgrade still a potential catalyst but not imminent.
instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver at $72.81, down 0.31% after -3% previous session. Up 123% YoY. Stalled Iran talks and Hormuz closure fueling inflation fears that may keep rates elevated, weighing on non-yielding metals. Goldman targets $85-100 avg, Citi targets $110 H2 2026.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold at $4,520. JP Morgan forecasts $5,055 Q4 2026, $5,400 by end 2027. Central banks buying 25-30% of global production for 3-4 years running. Gold ETFs saw record $19B inflows in Jan 2026. BNP targets $6,000 by year-end.
instrument_news.MSTR: At $161-172. Strategy holds 818,334 BTC. Filed for dual $21B ATM programs ($42B total capacity). Stock up 12.55% YTD. Analysts project $176-228 for 2026. Key risk: BTC correlation — MSTR trades as leveraged BTC play.

metals_outlook: Structural bull intact. Central bank demand continues at 25-30% of global production. Gold ETF inflows at record levels. Silver COT positioning still constructive. However, near-term headwind from elevated oil/inflation keeping rates higher-for-longer. Gold-silver ratio compressing but not as fast as silver bulls hoped. JP Morgan $5K gold Q4 2026, Citi $110 silver H2 2026. Key catalyst: any Fed pivot or rate cut signal would accelerate metals rally.
oil_outlook: Severe supply shock from Strait of Hormuz dual blockade (US and Iran). Brent at $118 (+6%), WTI at $107. IEA calls it largest supply shock on record — Hormuz handles 20% of global oil trade. Trump maintaining blockade until Iran nuclear deal. Extended disruption creates stagflation risk: high oil = inflation + growth drag. Watch for: 1) Any military escalation, 2) Iran peace talk resumption, 3) SPR release announcements, 4) OPEC+ response.
sentiment_shift: Risk-off intensifying. 5 straight Dow losses. Oil shock creating stagflation narrative. FOMC dissent adds uncertainty. However, F&G at 26 (fear zone) is historically contrarian bullish. Big Tech earnings tonight are the binary catalyst — strong results could reverse sentiment, weak results could accelerate selloff. Gold as safe haven thesis strengthening. Crypto showing resilience despite traditional market weakness.
